briefdescription.para: Given an uninitialized instance of a doubly-linked list (dllist)

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_init() is called
Then the list is initialized, has null pointers to the first and the last nodes

briefdescription.para: Given an uninitialized instance of a doubly-linked list node (dlnode)

detaileddescription.para: When dlnode_init() is called
Then the node is initialized, has null pointers to the next and the previous nodes

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_empty() is called
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with at least one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_empty() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_size() is called
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_size() is called
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with two nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_size() is called
Then 2 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_push_front() is called with a pointer to the node
Then the node is added to the list, the list is no longer empty

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_push_front() is called with a pointer to the node
Then the node is added to the list as the new first node, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_push_back() is called with a pointer to the node
Then the node is added to the list, the list is no longer empty

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_push_back() is called with a pointer to the node
Then the node is added to the list as the new last node, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_front() is called
Then a null pointer is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_front() is called
Then a pointer to the only node is returned, this node is removed from the list and the list is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_front() is called
Then a pointer to the first node is returned, this node is removed from the list and size of the list is decreased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_back() is called
Then a null pointer is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_back() is called
Then a pointer to the only node is returned, this node is removed from the list and the list is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_pop_back() is called
Then a pointer to the last node is returned, this node is removed from the list and size of the list is decreased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with two nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_insert_after() is called with a pointer to the first node in the list and a pointer to the initialized node
Then the initialized node is inserted into the list between the first and the second nodes of the list, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_insert_after() is called with a pointer to the last node in the list and a pointer to the initialized node
Then the initialized node is inserted into the list after the last node of the list, becoming the new last node of the list, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with two nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_insert_before() is called with a pointer to the second node in the list and a pointer to the initialized node
Then the initialized node is inserted into the list between the first and the second nodes of the list, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes and an initialized node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_insert_before() is called with a pointer to the first node in the list and a pointer to the initialized node
Then the initialized node is inserted into the list before the first node of the list, becoming the new first node of the list, size of the list is increased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_remove() is called with a pointer to the first node
Then the original first node is removed from the list, its successor is the new first node of the list, size of the list is decreased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_remove() is called with a pointer to the last node
Then the original last node is removed from the list, its predecessor is the new last node of the list, size of the list is decreased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_remove() is called with a pointer to one of the middle nodes
Then the requested node is removed from the list, its predecessor and successor are linked directly, size of the list is decreased by 1

briefdescription.para: Given two empty doubly-linked lists

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_append() is called with pointers to both lists
Then first pointer to list is returned, nothing is changed

briefdescription.para: Given two doubly-linked lists, one empty, the other with a single node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_remove() is called with two pointers, first to the empty list, second to the non-empty one
Then first pointer to list is returned, the originally empty list contains the only node from the other list, the other list is empty

briefdescription.para: Given two doubly-linked lists, one empty, the other with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_append() is called with two pointers, first to the empty list, second to the non-empty one
Then first pointer to list is returned, the originally empty list contains all nodes from the other list in the same order, the other list is empty

briefdescription.para: Given two doubly-linked lists, both with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_append() is called with pointers to both lists
Then first pointer to list is returned, this list contains all nodes from the other list in the same order placed right after the original nodes of this list, the other list is empty

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a null pointer
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the list
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a pointer to the node in the list
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with one node

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the list
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with two nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a pointer to one of the nodes in the list
Then 1 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with two nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_contains() is called with a pointer to a node not present in the list
Then 0 is returned

briefdescription.para: Given N/A

detaileddescription.para: When dlnode_foreach() is used with a null pointer
Then no loop iterations are performed

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dlnode_foreach() is used with a pointer to a middle node of the list
Then body of the loop is executed for requested node and all its successors

briefdescription.para: Given an empty doubly-linked list

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_foreach() is used
Then no loop iterations are performed

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_foreach() is used
Then body of the loop is executed for all nodes from the list in order from the first to the last

briefdescription.para: Given a doubly-linked list with multiple nodes

detaileddescription.para: When dllist_foreach() is used and one of the nodes is removed
Then body of the loop is executed for all nodes from the list and the removed node is not present in the list

briefdescription.para: This file is part of the CANopen Library Unit Test Suite.

detaileddescription.para:
2020-2021 N7 Space Sp. z o.o.
Unit Test Suite was developed under a programme of, and funded by, the European Space Agency.
